AN EF0 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 80MPH TOUCHED DOWN EAST OF CUBA AND TRACKED 0.67 MILES TO THE NORTHEAST ALONG THE NORFOLK SOUTHERN RR CAUSING MINOR TREE DAMAGE AND ROOF DAMAGE TO ONE HOME. PATH WIDTH 100 YDS.

A TORNADO MOVED PARALLEL TO COUTY ROAD 117 IN NORTHERN GREENE COUNTY. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF1 AS DOZENS OF HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED OFF OR WERE UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH. ONE HOME...ONE MOBILE HOME...AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGISTS SURVEYED DAMAGE IN GREENE AND TUSCALOOSA COUNTIES AND CONFIRMED A WEAK TORNADO. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE GREENE-TUSCALOOSA COUNTY LINE JUST SOUTH OF THE JENA COMMUNITY. THE INITIAL TOUCHDOWN WAS ALONG SHILOH RD WHERE TREES WERE UPROOTED. THE TORNADO TRAVELED NORTHEAST INTO TUSCALOOSA COUNTY CROSSING HOMESTEAD RD AND ROMULUS RD WHERE TWO HOMES SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN A PECAN GROVE WEST OF PEACHBURG PLANTATION...UPROOTING A MATURE HEALTHY PECAN TREE. IT TRAVELLED NORTHEAST PARALLELING PEACHBURG ROAD...SNAPPING AND UPROOTING NUMEROUS PINES AND HARDWOODS. IT CONTINUED ALONG A UTILITY RIGHT OF WAY BEFORE CROSSING PEACHBURG ROAD ROUGHLY A MILE FROM TOUCHDOWN...AND LIFTING JUST AFTER CROSSING THE ROAD. MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 150 YARDS AND THE TOTAL PATH LENGTH WAS 1.15 MILES. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND WAS 95 MPH.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN LEESBURG. ALSO DAMAGE TO A BARN AND HOUSE. CORRECTED REPORT.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN LEESBURG
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ALONG PLEASANT VALLEY RD
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ALONG PLEASANT VALLEY RD. ALSO DAMAGE TO AGRICULTURAL BUILDINGS AT PLEASANT VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL. UPDATED REPORT.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG KEENER DR JUST NORTHWEST OF THE CITY OF LEESBURG. MULTIPLE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED...AND UNFORTUNATELY ONE TREE FELL ONTO A RESIDENCE CAUSING SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE TO BOTH THE HOME AND VEHICLE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTH NORTHEAST CAUSING ADDITIONAL TREE DAMAGE BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CR 166 AND CR 670. MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS AND TOTAL PATH LENGTH WAS 3.5 MILES. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND WAS 80 MPH.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN A VERY RURAL PORTION OF SOUTHWEST RUSSELL COUNTY...NEAR THE BARBOUR COUNTY LINE ALONG PERRYS WELL ROAD. THE TORNADO TRAVELED NORTHEAST APPROXIMATELY ONE-HALF MILE BEFORE LIFTING. MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 188 YARDS. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND WAS 95 MPH.
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE REPORTED NEAR INTERSECTION OF HWY 165 AND OSWICHEE RD. EXTENT OF DAMAGE UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME. POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH OF COUNTY ROAD 18 JUST WEST OF HIGHWSY 165. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEAST AND CROSSED HIGHWAY 165...AND CAUSED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO SEVERAL BUSINESSES IN THE DROP ZONE MARKET...REMOVING THE ROOF OFF A METAL BUILDING AS WELL AS TOSSING SEVERAL ROOFTOP AIR CONDITIONING UNITS. A LARGE HARDWOOD TREE WAS ALSO UPROOTED IN THE SAME LOCATION. AS IT CONTINUED NORTHEAST...IT CROSSED TWO SUBDIVISIONS CAUSING MAINLY ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE TO HOMES IN THE SUBDIVISION.
